shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient align: Processor must inform the Controller of the legal requirement
results.condition_check: compatible: contract says 'in such a case' and parent clause covers the trigger where Processor is required by Union/Member State law; this matches the regulation's legal-prohibition condition
results.modality_check: equal force: 'shall' matches 'shall'
results.other_constraints_check: compatible: 'before Processing' matches 'before processing'
results.contract_source_text: to Process Personal Data only in accordance with the instructions from the Controller, cf. clause 2 of this Agreement, including with regard to transfers of Personal Data to a Third Country or an international organisation, unless required to do so by Union or Member State law to which the Processor is subject; in such a case, the Processor shall inform the Controller of that legal requirement before Processing, unless that law prohibits such information on important grounds of public interest;

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient align: the processor must inform the controller about intended changes concerning addition or replacement of sub-processors
results.condition_check: compatible: both are triggered in case of general written authorization/authorisation
results.modality_check: compatible: 'shall' on the contract side matches the regulatory obligation
results.other_constraints_check: no material differences in timing, manner, target, location, or cause
results.contract_source_text: In the case of general written authorisation, the Processor shall inform the Controller of any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, thereby giving the Controller the opportunity to object to such changes. However, the Controller cannot object to any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, if the new Sub-processor provides sufficient guarantees with respect to implementation of appropriate technical and organizational measures in such a manner that the Processing will meet the requirements of the GDPR as outlined in this Agreement. If the Controller does not object to changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors within 30 days from the Processor’s notification of the intended changes, such changes shall be deemed to be accepted by the Controller.

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object align: processor shall process personal data
results.condition_check: no condition on either side; compatible
results.modality_check: shall on contract side matches regulatory shall
results.other_constraints_check: only/on documented instructions from the controller matches on documented instructions from the controller; equivalent
results.contract_source_text: The Controller hereby authorises the Processor to Process the Personal Data on behalf of the Controller on the terms and conditions set out in this Agreement. The Processor shall Process the Personal Data only on documented instructions from the Controller. The Parties agree that this Agreement shall constitute the instructions as of the date of the Agreement. The Processor may – unless otherwise specifically is set out in the Agreement – apply all relevant means, including software, servers and IT systems.

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient align: both require the Processor to allow for and contribute to audits/inspections; the contract covers audits including inspections by an external qualified auditor mandated by the Controller, which is substantively the same audit-access obligation.
results.condition_check: No substantive condition in the regulation; the contract adds a confidentiality-related qualifier and purpose limitation, which is not weaker and does not conflict.
results.modality_check: Regulation is mandatory ('shall'); the contract uses a plain statement in a DPA obligation context, which is mandatory enough and not merely permissive.
results.other_constraints_check: Timing is stricter/compatible ('during normal business hours'); manner and purpose limitations do not make the duty looser than the regulation, and the recipient matches the controller-mandated auditor requirement.
results.contract_source_text: to allow for and contribute during normal business hours to reasonably necessary audits including inspections, conducted by an external qualified auditor mandated by the Controller, solely for the purpose of fulfilment of the Controller's obligations laid down in Article 28 of the GDPR and for accurately stipulated research questions in this connection provided that such external qualified auditor is subject to and bound by confidentiality obligations as stipulated in clause 12 of this Agreement;

results.core_alignment_check: Processor ensures that persons authorised to process personal data have committed themselves to confidentiality or are under an appropriate statutory obligation of confidentiality — same obligation and same actors/object.
results.condition_check: No condition in either side; compatible.
results.modality_check: Contract plain statement is mandatory in sub-item context and matches shall.
results.other_constraints_check: No manner/timing/target/location/cause constraints present; compatible.
results.contract_source_text: Processor ensures that persons authorised to process the Personal Data have committed themselves to confidentiality or are under an appropriate statutory obligation of confidentiality.

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient align: processor shall remain liable to controller for performance of sub-processor obligations; contract says 'its Sub-processor's obligations' but substantively the same liability duty
results.condition_check: No condition on either side
results.modality_check: shall matches shall
results.other_constraints_check: No timing/manner/target/location/cause constraints on either side
results.contract_source_text: Processor shall remain liable to the Controller for the performance of its Sub- processor's obligations.

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object align: the processor must implement appropriate technical and organisational measures in both; the contract also ties them to GDPR compliance and protection of data subjects' rights, which is substantively at least as protective as ensuring security appropriate to risk
results.condition_check: no condition in either side
results.modality_check: agrees to is an обязательство/commitment and is not weaker than shall
results.other_constraints_check: contract manner is compatible and at least as strong: ensuring GDPR compliance and protection of data subjects' rights is consistent with, and not looser than, ensuring security appropriate to risk
results.contract_source_text: The Processor agrees to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures in such a manner that the Processing of the Personal Data will meet the requirements of the GDPR and other applicable national and EU data protection law and ensure the protection of the rights of the Data Subjects.

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object align at a high level (processor liable for damages caused by processing), but the contract does not capture the same liability trigger or scope

results.contract_source_text: a process for testing, assessing and evaluating the effectiveness of technical and organisational measures for ensuring the security of the Processing. 2. In assessing the appropriate level of security account shall be taken in particular of the risks that are presented by Processing, in particular from accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorised disclosure of, or access to Personal Data transmitted, stored or otherwise processed. 3. The Processor hosts the data, including Personal Data, of the Controller via Microsoft Cloud Azure. The online hosting Services are delivered from data centres solely situated within EU, in Ireland and the Netherlands. 4. Microsoft Azure platform provides the security measures as described on www.Processor.com. 5. Particular security measures to be taken out by the Processor under this Agreement are specified in detail on www.Processor.com (Processor's Security and Privacy Protection Policies). results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the contract to include a process for regularly testing, assessing and evaluating the effectiveness of technical and organisational measures for security. The contract parent/sub-item only says that, in assessing the appropriate level of security, account shall be taken of risks presented by processing, and separately references a process for testing/assessing/evaluating in the original text but not as operative contract content. That is a different duty (risk assessment / security-level determination), not the same obligation to include a regular testing/evaluation process.
results.condition_check: The regulation requires the activity to be done regularly. The contract clause has no regular-testing timing obligation in its operative structured fields; it instead concerns taking account of risks when assessing security.
results.modality_check: The regulation is an inclusion/content requirement with can-level modality. The contract clause is mandatory in form, but it does not impose the same content obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: No compatible timing or other substantive constraint matching the required regular testing/evaluating process is present; the clause focuses on risk factors for security assessment rather than the testing/evaluation process itself.
results.contract_source_text: a process for testing, assessing and evaluating the effectiveness of technical and organisational measures for ensuring the security of the Processing. 2. The Processor hosts the data, including Personal Data, of the Controller via Microsoft Cloud Azure. The online hosting Services are delivered from data centres solely situated within EU, in Ireland and the Netherlands. 4. results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object match: the Processor shall process personal data; same obligation as processing only on documented instructions from the controller

results.contract_source_text: to Process Personal Data only in accordance with the instructions from the Controller, cf. clause 2 of this Agreement, including with regard to transfers of Personal Data to a Third Country or an international organisation, unless required to do so by Union or Member State law to which the Processor is subject; in such a case, the Processor shall inform the Controller of that legal requirement before Processing, unless that law prohibits such information on important grounds of public interest;

results.core_alignment_check: The regulation prohibits the processor from transferring personal data to a third country or international organization without prior specific or general authorization from the controller. The contract parent/sub-item requires the processor to process personal data only in accordance with the controller's instructions, including with regard to transfers to a third country or international organisation. This is the same underlying transfer-control obligation and is at least as strict, because transfers are only allowed under controller instructions and the clause is framed restrictively.
results.condition_check: Compatible: the contract's exception for being required by Union or Member State law does not weaken the controller-authorization requirement; it is an additional lawful-exception qualifier and does not permit unauthorized transfers beyond the regulation's scope.
results.modality_check: Compatible: the regulation is prohibitive ('shall not'); the contract is mandatory and restrictive ('shall only ... including with regard to transfers'), which is equal-or-greater force.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the contract specifically covers transfers of personal data to a third country or international organisation, matching the regulation's target/recipient and not adding a contradictory timing, manner, or location limitation.
results.contract_source_text: to Process Personal Data only in accordance with the instructions from the Controller, cf. clause 2 of this Agreement, including with regard to transfers of Personal Data to a Third Country or an international organisation, unless required to do so by Union or Member State law to which the Processor is subject; in such a case, the Processor shall inform the Controller of that legal requirement before Processing, unless that law prohibits such information on important grounds of public interest;

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object align on the processor implementing appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure security appropriate to risk; contract is joint controller+processor and thus at least as broad as processor-only
results.condition_check: Compatible: contract adds the standard Art. 32 factors (state of the art, costs, nature/scope/context/purposes, risk) which are consistent with and narrower/stricter than the regulation's risk-based trigger
results.modality_check: Compatible: 'shall' matches the regulatory obligation
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the contract's security-risk formulation and 'including inter alia as appropriate' are substantively equivalent and not weaker than the regulation's manner requirement
results.contract_source_text: Data relating to criminal convictions and offences (cf. GDPR art. 10). *Sensitive data includes: data revealing racial or ethnic origin, political opinions, religious and/or philosophical beliefs, trade union membership, processing of genetic data or biometric data for the purpose of uniquely identifying a natural person, data concerning health and/or data concerning a natural person's sex life or sexual orientation Please note that for the processing of sensitive data, explicit consent of the Data Subject is required. Appendix 2 Security of Processing This Appendix forms part of the Agreement. 1. Taking into account the state of the art, the costs of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of Processing as well as the risk of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the Controller and the Processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including inter alia as appropriate: (a) encryption of Personal Data when transmitted via public networks and in connection with remote access to Controller’s systems; (b) the ability to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of Processing systems and Services;

Taking into account the state of the art, the costs of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of Processing as well as the risk of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the Controller and the Processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including inter alia as appropriate: (a) encryption of Personal Data when transmitted via public networks and in connection with remote access to Controller’s systems; (b) the ability to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of Processing systems and Services;

results.contract_source_text: The Controller hereby authorises the Processor to Process the Personal Data on behalf of the Controller on the terms and conditions set out in this Agreement. The Processor shall Process the Personal Data only on documented instructions from the Controller. The Parties agree that this Agreement shall constitute the instructions as of the date of the Agreement. The Processor may – unless otherwise specifically is set out in the Agreement – apply all relevant means, including software, servers and IT systems.

results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the processor to be liable for damage caused by processing only in the specified GDPR-breach / unlawful-instructions circumstances. results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the processor to be allowed to process personal data without documented instructions when required by Union or Member State law. results.condition_check: The regulation's trigger is 'if required by Union or Member State law'; the contract has no corresponding legal-law exception and instead ties processing to documented instructions under the agreement.

results.contract_source_text: The Controller hereby authorises the Processor to Process the Personal Data on behalf of the Controller on the terms and conditions set out in this Agreement. The Processor shall Process the Personal Data only on documented instructions from the Controller. The Parties agree that this Agreement shall constitute the instructions as of the date of the Agreement. The Processor may – unless otherwise specifically is set out in the Agreement – apply all relevant means, including software, servers and IT systems.

results.modality_check: Compatible: the contract's 'shall' is stronger than the regulation's 'can include'.

results.contract_source_text: Data relating to criminal convictions and offences (cf. GDPR art. 10). *Sensitive data includes: data revealing racial or ethnic origin, political opinions, religious and/or philosophical beliefs, trade union membership, processing of genetic data or biometric data for the purpose of uniquely identifying a natural person, data concerning health and/or data concerning a natural person's sex life or sexual orientation Please note that for the processing of sensitive data, explicit consent of the Data Subject is required. Appendix 2 Security of Processing This Appendix forms part of the Agreement. 1. Taking into account the state of the art, the costs of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of Processing as well as the risk of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the Controller and the Processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including inter alia as appropriate: (a) encryption of Personal Data when transmitted via public networks and in connection with remote access to Controller’s systems; (b) the ability to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of Processing systems and Services;

Appendix 2 Security of Processing This Appendix forms part of the Agreement. 1. Taking into account the state of the art, the costs of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of Processing as well as the risk of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the Controller and the Processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including inter alia as appropriate: (a) encryption of Personal Data when transmitted via public networks and in connection with remote access to Controller’s systems; (b) the ability to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of Processing systems and Services;

results.contract_source_text: The Controller hereby authorises the Processor to Process the Personal Data on behalf of the Controller on the terms and conditions set out in this Agreement. The Processor may – unless otherwise specifically is set out in the Agreement – apply all relevant means, including software, servers and IT systems.

results.core_alignment_check: No: the regulation requires a measure to restore availability and access to personal data in a timely manner after a physical or technical incident, while the contract parent/sub-item only requires implementing security measures and includes testing/evaluating effectiveness and ensuring ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience; it does not specifically require restoration of availability/access after an incident.
results.condition_check: The contract condition is a general security-risk context, not the specific event of a physical or technical incident triggering restoration.
results.modality_check: The contract is mandatory, but it mandates different security measures rather than the restoration obligation itself.
results.other_constraints_check: The timing/target substance is missing: there is no requirement to restore availability and access in a timely manner in the event of an incident.

If the Controller does not object to changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors within 30 days from the Processor’s notification of the intended changes, such changes shall be deemed to be accepted by the Controller.

results.contract_source_text: The Processor shall use reasonable endeavours to comply with any legislative changes. However, the Processor shall not be obligated to implement any change of the instructions if the Parties cannot in good faith agree to reasonable terms for the implementation. If the Parties fail to agree in good faith to reasonable terms regarding change of the instructions each Party shall be entitled to terminate this Agreement with a written notice of 60 days, provided that such changes are deemed necessary to comply with the GDPR or other applicable EU or national data protection laws and regulation. The Main Agreement and any other agreement between the Parties involving Processing of Personal Data shall automatically terminate at the same time.

results.contract_source_text: As part of the Main Agreement the Controller may also choose to use third party integration services and/or applications made available by the Processor on Processor.com in cooperation with the third party providers of such integration services and applications. If the Controller uses such third party integration services and/or applications it hereby authorises the Processor to: i) provide, transmit or transfer the data, including Personal Data, of the Controller to the third party provider of the relevant integration service and/or application, provided and only to the extent this is necessary for the performance and use by Controller of the said integration services and/or applications, and ii) Process data, including Personal Data, of the Controller that are transferred from the third party provider of the relevant integration service and/or application to the Controller’s Services with Processor.

results.contract_source_text: Data relating to criminal convictions and offences (cf. GDPR art. 10). *Sensitive data includes: data revealing racial or ethnic origin, political opinions, religious and/or philosophical beliefs, trade union membership, processing of genetic data or biometric data for the purpose of uniquely identifying a natural person, data concerning health and/or data concerning a natural person's sex life or sexual orientation Please note that for the processing of sensitive data, explicit consent of the Data Subject is required. Appendix 2 Security of Processing This Appendix forms part of the Agreement. 1. Taking into account the state of the art, the costs of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of Processing as well as the risk of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the Controller and the Processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including inter alia as appropriate: (a) encryption of Personal Data when transmitted via public networks and in connection with remote access to Controller’s systems; (b) the ability to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of Processing systems and Services;

results.contract_source_text: The Controller hereby authorises the Processor to Process the Personal Data on behalf of the Controller on the terms and conditions set out in this Agreement. The Processor shall Process the Personal Data only on documented instructions from the Controller. The Parties agree that this Agreement shall constitute the instructions as of the date of the Agreement. The Processor may – unless otherwise specifically is set out in the Agreement – apply all relevant means, including software, servers and IT systems.

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object do not match: regulation requires the processor to ensure persons under its authority with access to personal data process only on controller instructions; contract instead concerns using reasonable endeavours to comply with legislative changes

results.contract_source_text: The Processor shall use reasonable endeavours to comply with any legislative changes. However, the Processor shall not be obligated to implement any change of the instructions if the Parties cannot in good faith agree to reasonable terms for the implementation. If the Parties fail to agree in good faith to reasonable terms regarding change of the instructions each Party shall be entitled to terminate this Agreement with a written notice of 60 days, provided that such changes are deemed necessary to comply with the GDPR or other applicable EU or national data protection laws and regulation. The Main Agreement and any other agreement between the Parties involving Processing of Personal Data shall automatically terminate at the same time.

results.contract_source_text: In the case of general written authorisation, the Processor shall inform the Controller of any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, thereby giving the Controller the opportunity to object to such changes. However, the Controller cannot object to any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, if the new Sub-processor provides sufficient guarantees with respect to implementation of appropriate technical and organizational measures in such a manner that the Processing will meet the requirements of the GDPR as outlined in this Agreement. If the Controller does not object to changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors within 30 days from the Processor’s notification of the intended changes, such changes shall be deemed to be accepted by the Controller.

results.contract_source_text: In the case of general written authorisation, the Processor shall inform the Controller of any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, thereby giving the Controller the opportunity to object to such changes. However, the Controller cannot object to any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, if the new Sub-processor provides sufficient guarantees with respect to implementation of appropriate technical and organizational measures in such a manner that the Processing will meet the requirements of the GDPR as outlined in this Agreement. If the Controller does not object to changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors within 30 days from the Processor’s notification of the intended changes, such changes shall be deemed to be accepted by the Controller.

Appendix 2 Security of Processing This Appendix forms part of the Agreement. 1. Taking into account the state of the art, the costs of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of Processing as well as the risk of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the Controller and the Processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including inter alia as appropriate: (a) encryption of Personal Data when transmitted via public networks and in connection with remote access to Controller’s systems; (b) the ability to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of Processing systems and Services;

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient align: both require the processor to inform the controller about intended changes concerning addition/replacement of sub-processors; the contract parent clause also gives the controller an opportunity to object, which is additional but does not negate the notice duty

results.contract_source_text: In the case of general written authorisation, the Processor shall inform the Controller of any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, thereby giving the Controller the opportunity to object to such changes. However, the Controller cannot object to any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, if the new Sub-processor provides sufficient guarantees with respect to implementation of appropriate technical and organizational measures in such a manner that the Processing will meet the requirements of the GDPR as outlined in this Agreement. If the Controller does not object to changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors within 30 days from the Processor’s notification of the intended changes, such changes shall be deemed to be accepted by the Controller.

results.modality_check: plain statement under a mandatory parent does not weaken, but modality is irrelevant because the obligation is different
results.other_constraints_check: no substantive overlap on manner/timing/target/location/cause; the contract clause is a different subject matter and does not supply the required controller identity/contact details
results.contract_source_text: specific instruction from the Controller.

results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires that the contract include organizational and technical measures ensuring security. The contract parent/sub-item includes a process for testing, assessing and evaluating the effectiveness of technical and organisational measures for ensuring the security of the Processing, and also references security measures generally. This is the same security-measures obligation, with at least one listed measure matching the regulation's examples.
results.condition_check: No condition in either side that would weaken or conflict.
results.modality_check: The contract uses a mandatory parent/sub-item context and a descriptive statement within that mandatory clause; this is not weaker than the regulation's 'can include' formulation.
results.other_constraints_check: The content is substantively aligned on security measures and testing/evaluating effectiveness; no timing, manner, target, location, or cause conflict.
results.contract_source_text: a process for testing, assessing and evaluating the effectiveness of technical and organisational measures for ensuring the security of the Processing. 2. In assessing the appropriate level of security account shall be taken in particular of the risks that are presented by Processing, in particular from accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorised disclosure of, or access to Personal Data transmitted, stored or otherwise processed. 3. The Processor hosts the data, including Personal Data, of the Controller via Microsoft Cloud Azure. The online hosting Services are delivered from data centres solely situated within EU, in Ireland and the Netherlands. 4. Microsoft Azure platform provides the security measures as described on www.Processor.com. 5. Particular security measures to be taken out by the Processor under this Agreement are specified in detail on www.Processor.com (Processor's Security and Privacy Protection Policies). results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient do not describe the same obligation: the regulation requires including pseudonymisation and encryption as security measures, while the contract defines 'Personal Data Breach' as a security incident involving destruction/loss/alteration/disclosure/access
results.condition_check: No condition in either side
results.modality_check: Regulation is a permissive inclusion ('can include'); contract is a definition with 'shall mean', but it does not impose the required measure
results.other_constraints_check: No relevant timing/manner/target/location/cause alignment; the contract addresses a different concept entirely
results.contract_source_text: ‘Personal Data Breach’ shall mean a breach of security leading to the accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorised disclosure of, or access to, Personal Data transmitted, stored or otherwise Processed;

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object do not match: the regulation requires the DPA to contain at least one controller identity and contact details, while the contract clause only states governing law for the legal relationship between Controller and Processor.
results.condition_check: No condition in either side.
results.modality_check: The contract is a plain governing-law statement, not a content obligation to include controller identity/contact details.
results.other_constraints_check: No relevant timing/manner/target/location/cause overlap.
results.contract_source_text: The legal relationship between Controller and Processor is exclusively governed by the laws of Denmark without regard to its principles of conflicts of law. Disputes between parties will, in the first instance, be exclusively resolved by the District Court of Copenhagen, Denmark.

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient do not describe the same obligation: the regulation requires the DPA to contain the categories of data subjects, while the contract authorizes processing/transfer by the Processor and does not state any categories of data subjects
results.condition_check: No relevant condition overlap; the contract condition about use of third-party integration services is unrelated to listing categories of data subjects.
results.modality_check: The contract uses an authorization/permission structure, but modality is not the main issue because the subject matter is different; in any event it does not impose the required content obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: No timing/manner/target/location/cause overlap relevant to the required content.
results.contract_source_text: As part of the Main Agreement the Controller may also choose to use third party integration services and/or applications made available by the Processor on Processor.com in cooperation with the third party providers of such integration services and applications. If the Controller uses such third party integration services and/or applications it hereby authorises the Processor to: i) provide, transmit or transfer the data, including Personal Data, of the Controller to the third party provider of the relevant integration service and/or application, provided and only to the extent this is necessary for the performance and use by Controller of the said integration services and/or applications, and ii) Process data, including Personal Data, of the Controller that are transferred from the third party provider of the relevant integration service and/or application to the Controller’s Services with Processor.
